item.title: U.S. launches more Iran strikes as threats to oil trade deepen
item.description: President Trump has threatened to bomb an Iranian bridge or power plant for every ship that's attacked in the Strait of Hormuz, as one shipping insider says "nothing is going through."

item.title: Council handpicked by Trump to gut review process of historic-preservation projects
item.description: The Advisory Council for Historic Preservation, whose members were mostly handpicked by President Trump, is poised to end mandatory state review and public comment on projects affecting historical properties.

item.title: Sneak a peek at this holiday season's hottest new toys
item.description: While the holiday season is still months away, it's already prime time for the toy industry. Bradley Blackburn got a look at some of the new items that could be on kids' lists in December.

item.title: New technology could restore movement, sensation for millions of paralyzed patients
item.description: New findings identified a technology that could restore movement and sensation for millions of paralyzed people living with spinal cord injuries. Bradley Blackburn has the story.

item.title: Trump and Iran trade threats to attack energy infrastructure as U.S. completes 11th night of strikes
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday said that for every Iranian drone or missile attack in the Strait of Hormuz, the U.S. will "bomb and destroy one bridge or power plant." Iran said if the U.S. hits civilian targets it will seek to halt all oil flow from the region. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Samantha Vinograd have the latest.

item.title: U.S. and Saudi Arabia sign nuclear cooperation deal
item.description: The U.S. and Saudi Arabia signed a deal for U.S. companies to run nuclear power on Saudi soil, the U.S. Department of Energy announced Wednesday.

item.title: House GOP advances $95 billion plan to fund Iran war and SAVE America Act
item.description: House Republicans adopted a budget resolution that kickstarts the party-line reconciliation process. It now heads to the Senate, where it'll face an uphill battle.

item.title: OpenAI says its new model hacked another company on its own
item.description: OpenAI says its new artificial intelligence model hacked another company on its own in what the company called "an unprecedented cyber incident." Mashable tech editor Tim Werth joins CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Melinda French Gates explains why she tracks her blood work in a spreadsheet
item.description: Melinda French Gates told Norah O'Donnell on "Healthful" that she's been tracking all her blood work in a spreadsheet "for a very long time." "It's easier than when I sit down with my doctor and she's pacing through the computer to look at it. I'm like, here's the spreadsheet," French Gates said.

item.title: First beluga whales from Marineland arrive in Texas as rescue underway
item.description: Two beluga whales settled into their new home at SeaWorld San Antonio this week following an international relocation mission from shuttered Canadian theme park Marineland.

item.title: Secret Service confident in security measures for rescheduled White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: The Secret Service held a briefing on Wednesday ahead of Friday's rescheduled White House Correspondents' Dinner. The event was postponed in April after shots broke out near the screening area. CBS News homeland security correspondent Nicole Sganga reports.

item.title: Early details on U.S.-Saudi Arabia nuclear deal
item.description: The U.S. and Saudi Arabia are sealing the deal on a civilian nuclear program. Wall Street Journal national security correspondent Michael Gordon joins CBS News to discuss his reporting on the agreement.

item.title: Inside the transfer of beluga whales from a closed Canadian theme park to U.S. aquariums
item.description: Dozens of beluga whales are being transferred from a closed Canadian theme park to a number of aquariums across the United States. CBS News reporter Jared Ochacher has more.

item.title: House passes bill to limit stock purchases by members of Congress
item.description: The Stop Insider Trading Act would prohibit members of Congress, their spouses and dependent children from purchasing publicly traded stocks.

item.title: ChatGPT's medical advice nearly killed a man, lawsuit claims
item.description: The case accuses OpenAI of practicing medicine without a license and failing to safeguard users seeking health advice.

item.title: Trump speaks at Georgia high school after attending dignified transfer
item.description: President Trump spoke at a high school in Marietta, Georgia, on Wednesday, after attending a dignified transfer for four U.S. troops killed in the Middle East. CBS News congressional reporter Taurean Small has the latest.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha makes landfall in Louisiana
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha made landfall in Louisiana on Wednesday afternoon. CBS News meteorologist Jessica Burch has the latest on the storm's projected path.

item.title: What to know about Taylor Farms amid the cyclospora outbreak
item.description: The nation's largest recent cyclospora outbreak has put one of America's biggest produce suppliers under renewed scrutiny.

item.title: Taylor Farms pulls iceberg lettuce linked to cyclosporiasis outbreak
item.description: Taylor Farms said none of its branded salads or kits contain the iceberg lettuce associated with the outbreak.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha makes landfall on Gulf Coast. See its path.
item.description: Tropical Storm Bertha is the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season.

item.title: Waltz clashes with Rep. Jackson over Cuba question: "Absolute propaganda"
item.description: U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations Mike Waltz testified Wednesday in front of the House Foreign Affairs Committee, where he and Rep. Jonathan L. Jackson had a tense exchange on the current U.S.-Cuba situation.

item.title: Latest on U.S.-Saudi nuclear deal, Trump threats against Iranian infrastructure
item.description: CBS News National security analyst Aaron MacLean breaks down President Trump's threats against Iranian infrastructure and the United States' new nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia.

item.title: How much interest will a $40,000 1-year CD account earn if opened now?
item.description: A $40,000 1-year CD account will produce a substantial return for savers (and it will be available by next summer).

item.title: Democrats probe DOJ opinion that eases institutionalization of mentally ill
item.description: Democrats in the Senate and House are investigating the origins of a DOJ legal opinion that would make it easier for states to keep people with mental illness in institutions, rather than helping them to live at home.

item.title: Nolan Wells' cause of death undetermined, family's autopsy shows
item.description: Nolan Wells, 18, was found dead just off Horn Island in Mississippi after enjoying a day out with friends on July 4.

item.title: DOJ sues 2 Chinese companies over immersion water heaters that can ignite
item.description: The Justice Department is trying to force the two Chinese companies to recall immersion water heaters used for bathtubs and pools.

item.title: Trump approves nuclear deal that could allow Saudi Arabia to enrich uranium
item.description: President Trump approved a nuclear deal that could allow Saudi Arabia to enrich uranium, the Associated Press reports. CBS News' Courtney Kealy has more details.

item.title: OpenAI says its technology, on its own, hacked another AI company
item.description: ChatGPT maker OpenAI says its artificial intelligence system hacked into another AI company on its own in what the company called an "unprecedented cyber incident."

item.title: Pentagon identifies 4th service member killed as U.S.-Iran war escalates
item.description: The U.S. launched another night of strikes on Iran after four U.S. soldiers were killed in Jordan and Iraq in recent days.

item.title: U.S. soldier killed in Iraq identified as 30-year-old from North Carolina
item.description: Sgt. Michael Emmanuel Swinton, 30, of North Carolina, was "decommissioning ammunition and an accident happened," Secretary of State Marco Rubio said.
